name: Credit Repair Atlanta
description_short: Credit Repair Atlanta disputes inaccurate items on credit reports across Atlanta metro area. They claim to have removed late payments, tax liens, bankruptcies, and charge-offs for over 500,000 clients.
description_long:
Credit Repair Atlanta operates as a credit repair service based in the Atlanta, Georgia area, serving multiple suburbs including Sandy Springs, Roswell, Johns Creek, Alpharetta, Marietta, Dunwoody, Milton, Peachtree City, Kennesaw, Woodstock, Decatur, and Suwanee. The company positions itself as an industry leader in credit repair, claiming to have spent nine years helping clients remove negative items from their credit reports. They advertise that over 500,000 clients have chosen their services, though this figure is not independently verified on the website.

Credit Repair Atlanta's core service involves disputing inaccurate items on credit reports by leveraging consumer rights under federal laws including the FCRA (Fair Credit Reporting Act), HIPAA, and FCBA (Fair Credit Billing Act). Their stated process includes three phases: cleaning the past by removing inaccurate items, rebuilding the present through credit education and custom restoration plans, and restoring the future through credit navigation and financial guidance. They offer services related to late payments, tax liens, bankruptcies, charge-offs, student loans, repossessions, collections, and other negative items. The company also provides debt settlement, real estate credit repair, credit limit increases, credit monitoring, and general credit help.

The company distinguishes itself through a structured timeline process: initial credit report evaluation within 5 days, distribution of a credit survival guide within 10 days, and expected bureau responses within 30-45 days. They provide free initial credit report evaluation and claim to handle all correspondence with credit bureaus on behalf of clients. Their service model emphasizes personalized guidance through "Credit Navigators" and custom restoration plans tailored to individual situations.

While the website contains informative content about how credit scores work and why credit repair matters, several limitations should be noted. The company makes broad claims about client success and volume without third-party verification or specific case studies. No information is provided about pricing, contracts, typical timeframes for results, or success rates for specific negative items. The website does not display business licensing, certifications, or regulatory compliance details. No customer reviews or testimonials appear on the provided website content. The company's claims of removing items "permanently" may overstate typical outcomes, as credit repair results vary significantly based on individual circumstances.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Free initial credit report evaluation from all three bureaus (TransUnion, Experian, Equifax)
Claims to handle bureau correspondence directly on client's behalf, reducing client burden
Structured timeline process with clear expectations (5 days to first investigation, 30-45 day bureau response window)
Services cover wide range of negative items: late payments, tax liens, bankruptcies, charge-offs, student loans, repossessions, collections
Offers credit education and custom restoration plans, not just dispute services
Serves multiple Atlanta metro suburbs with localized service
Provides credit survival guide to help clients manage credit during repair process
cons: No pricing information disclosed on website; consumers cannot determine costs before engaging
Claims of removing items 'permanently' lack documentation or success rate data to support assertions
No verifiable third-party reviews, testimonials, or case studies provided on website
Broad claim of '500,000 clients' is unverified and lacks independent corroboration
No information about business licensing, certifications, bonding, or regulatory compliance displayed
best_for: Atlanta-area residents with inaccurate negative items on credit reports who want dispute assistance
Consumers with multiple negative items (bankruptcies, tax liens, collections) seeking comprehensive credit cleanup
People seeking credit education and guidance alongside dispute services, not just transactional disputes
diagnosis: Best for Atlanta metro consumers who have inaccurate negative items on their credit reports and want professional dispute assistance combined with credit education. Main caveat: lack of pricing transparency, unverified client volume claims, and no published success rates or independent reviews make it difficult to evaluate whether this company delivers results worth the service cost.
services: Free credit report evaluation (all three bureaus)
Credit dispute services under FCRA compliance
Credit education and custom restoration plans
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 830 M.L.K. Jr Dr NW, Atlanta, GA 30314
